title: ICS-300: Intermediate Incident Command
description: ICS-300 is a 3-day instructor-led course that builds on ICS-100 and ICS-200 fundamentals. Participants practice managing expanding incidents, supervising subordinate ICS positions, and coordinating interagency resources. Prerequisites: completion of ICS-100 and ICS-200 (or G-200). Attire: business casual. Lunch and parking provided at the Frankfort training facility.

title: ICS-400: Advanced Incident Command
description: ICS-400 is an advanced 2-day course designed for personnel who require a more thorough understanding of ICS as used for large-scale and/or complex incidents. Focuses on organizational complexities, unified command, and area command. Prerequisites: ICS-100, ICS-200, IS-700, IS-800, ICS-300. Travel and lodging are the participant's responsibility.

title: NIMS Resource Management
description: This half-day virtual course covers NIMS resource management principles including resource typing, credentialing, and inventory systems. Ideal for county EM staff responsible for managing resources during activations. Participants need a computer with a camera and a stable internet connection. Microsoft Teams link will be emailed 48 hours prior.

title: Public Information Officer Basics
description: A 2-day foundational course for local, state, and tribal government employees who work with media during emergencies. Topics include writing press releases, conducting briefings, managing social media, and integrating into the JIS/JIC structure. Materials included. No prerequisites.

title: Mass Care & Sheltering
description: A comprehensive 2-day course covering mass care shelter operations, feeding operations, bulk distribution, and reunification. Students practice using the Red Cross Shelter Management information system. Prerequisites: IS-100, IS-700. Meals and materials provided.

title: EOC Operations Workshop
description: An intensive 1-day workshop for EOC directors and section chiefs. Covers EOC activation levels, coordination with field ICS, WebEOC/Veoci workflows, and ESF coordination. Scenario-based exercises included. Limited to 20 participants — register early.

title: Damage Assessment & Debris Mgmt
description: This 2-day course covers field damage assessment procedures, debris estimation, FEMA PDA documentation, and debris monitoring contract oversight. Intended for county EM, public works, and road department personnel. Prerequisites: IS-100, IS-700.

title: Volunteer & Donations Management
description: A 1-day course covering coordination with volunteer organizations (VOAD), spontaneous volunteer management, donations management, and FEMA guidance on accepting disaster donations. Open to all EM and partner agency staff.

title: Community Lifelines Practicum
description: A 2-day practicum applying FEMA's Community Lifelines framework to tabletop exercise scenarios. Participants develop and present stabilization plans for each lifeline. Intended for experienced EM professionals. Prerequisites: completion of the Community Lifelines IS course.

title: THIRA/SPR Annual Workshop
description: Annual 1-day facilitated workshop for county and state planners to update Threat and Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment (THIRA) and Stakeholder Preparedness Review (SPR) submissions. Participation is required for EMPG compliance. Contact your regional coordinator to confirm attendance.

title: Winter Weather Preparedness
description: A half-day webinar reviewing Kentucky winter weather operations, NWS alert criteria, road condition monitoring, and shelter activation checklists. Intended for county EM directors and deputies. Recorded session available after the event.

title: After-Action Review Facilitation
description: A 1-day facilitated workshop on conducting After-Action Reviews and writing Improvement Plans following exercises or real events. Covers HSEEP AAR/IP templates and common facilitation pitfalls. Open to all exercise coordinators and EM staff.
